# Kite-collector log code catalog

<!-- Generated by `go run ./tools/loginventory`. Do not edit by hand. -->

Every kite-collector log call emits a `"code"` structured attribute. Downstream tooling (Loki/Splunk queries, SIEM alert rules, on-call runbooks) pivots on the code rather than parsing freeform message text — the code is the **stable identifier**, the message is informational.

Convention: `<namespace>.<surface>.<event>`. Codes are immutable once shipped — renaming breaks any alert that filters on the old value. To deprecate a code, add a new one and update the call site; never rename in place.

To regenerate this file:

```
go run ./tools/loginventory > docs/LOG_CODES.md
```

**Catalog size:** 306 codes across 21 packages.

---

**--- Cross-provider DNS-discovery shared events ------------------ Zone + record "discovered" entries are emitted by every provider in the same shape (so dashboards can sum across providers via `code IN (...)` filters). The provider is always available in the structured fields for per-provider breakdowns.**

| Code | Constant | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `cloud.dns.zone_discovered` | `LogCodeDNSZoneDiscovered` | — |
| `cloud.dns.record_discovered` | `LogCodeDNSRecordDiscovered` | — |

**--- retry helper (retry.go) ------------------------------------- The retry wrapper logs at distinct stages so alerting can fire independently on "we're retrying a lot" vs "access denied is permanent" vs "throttled by upstream".**

| Code | Constant | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `cloud.retry.backoff` | `LogCodeRetryBackoff` | — |
| `cloud.retry.network_error` | `LogCodeRetryNetworkError` | — |
| `cloud.retry.access_denied` | `LogCodeRetryAccessDenied` | — |
| `cloud.retry.rate_limited` | `LogCodeRetryRateLimited` | — |
| `cloud.retry.server_error` | `LogCodeRetryServerError` | — |
